zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda Pembridge於1345年出生在英國威爾斯蒙姆爾蒙姆斯時，她的父親Sir Robert de Pembridge of Tong, Knight35歲，母親Juliana la Zouche31歲。她於大約1368年與Thomas Norbury在英國英格蘭爾馬布里諾波利 亞爾 馬爾帕斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭爾。

nameMeanings.name: Pembridge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Pembridge (Herefs), which is recorded as Penebrug(g)e from the 11th to 14th centuries. The place-name derives from an Old English personal name * Pena + Old English brycg ‘bridge’. In some cases the surname may be from Pen Bridge in Shipley (Sussex), which derives from an uncertain first element + Old English brycg.

variants.name: Pember
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Pamber, a habitational name from a place in Hampshire named Pamber, from Old English penn ‘fold, enclosure’ + beorg ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda Pembridge was born in 1345, in Monmouth, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Robert de Pembridge of Tong, Knight, was 35 and her mother, Juliana la Zouche, was 31. She married Thomas Norbury about 1368, in Norbury near Malpas, Marbury, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Thomas Norbury environ 1368, à Norbury near Malpas, Marbury, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.<Norbury, Marbury, Cheshire, England>: 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 마버이 노르버이 니아어 맬파스
ko.lifeSummary: Matilda Pembridge(이)가 1345년 영국 웨일스 몬모우스실르 몬모우스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert de Pembridge of Tong, Knight(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Juliana la Zouche(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 대략 1368년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 마버이 노르버이 니아어 맬파스에서 Thomas Norbury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda Pembridge родилась в 1345 г. в местности монмоут, монмоутшир, Уэльс, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Robert de Pembridge of Tong, Knight было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Juliana la Zouche, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Norbury около 1368 г. в местности норбури нир малпас, марбури, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания.
